backend.changelog: Polish: (1) CAD Bridge status pill + transient SW status moved out of the main action toolbar into a dedicated thin row below it. (2) Benign status chips auto-hide after 15s; warning chips persist until replaced. (3) The CopyIdChip button added in 1.1.10/1.1.11 is removed; the EIN cell + drawing rows + BOM root header now have a hover panel showing EIN + Name + Title + id with Copy id button when Settings -> Show debug IDs is ON.

cadBridge.changelog: RefreshCache (engage path) primary open is now by EngItem PLMID instead of by filename. Previously the agent's ActivateByNameAndWait hardcoded the .SLDASM extension + docType==2 swDocASSEMBLY in three retry stages, so a Part-as-root BOM (PIN-001628 on disk: Shaft_DL_&.SLDPRT) always failed with 'Cannot open assembly PIN-001628 in SolidWorks' -- 30+ seconds of wasted retries even when SW already had the part loaded via the parallel openFrom3DX call. Same failure applied to 3DX-Connector cached docs whose opaque DOC-XXXXX.SLDPRT disk filename never matched the BOM title. Fix: new OpenRootByPlmidAndWait helper takes precedence when the frontend supplies assemblyId (it always does). Uses OpenDoc7 + PLMObjectSpecification.PLMID with DocumentType=1 then =2 retry, mirroring the BOM-driven loop's TryOpenByPlmid mechanic (1.8.43+). Pre-scans open docs so an already-loaded root is a no-op (no OpenDoc7 round-trip). Legacy filename-based path stays as fallback for older frontends. Workstation Apply Update needed.
